$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports/selectors/styles verbatim.

TypeScript port. Omits the blob-URL upload effect (useUploadMediaFromBlobURL), the gallery-replacement code path for multi-file selection, the link-destination resolution against window.wp.media.view.settings.defaultProps.link, and the duotone/border style hook usage. Render path (figure → Image → maxWidthObserver) preserved.

Simplified TypeScript port of upstream image.js (~1300 LOC). Toolbar uses MediaReplaceFlow only; inspector uses ToolsPanel with alt-text + title. Drops upstream's DimensionsTool, ResolutionTool, ImageURLInputUI, ImageEditor (cropper), FocalPointPicker, ResizableBox, lightbox toggle UI, MediaControl, and BlockSettingsMenuControls — all depend on block-library/private-apis internals blocked by the package exports field. The serialized markup is still produced by save.tsx so the divergence is editor-only.
TypeScript port; behaviour byte-equivalent to upstream including border/shadow/focalPoint serialization.
Full v1–v8 deprecation chain ported verbatim with their migrate/isEligible callbacks.
Upstream raw (figure → img) + files + caption-shortcode transforms preserved; added bidirectional core/image ↔ artisanpack/image block transforms.

Sass caption-style() and caption-style-theme() mixins inlined; $grid-unit-20/$border-width/$white literals inlined; lightbox keyframes preserved verbatim; data-type selectors duplicated for both core/image and artisanpack/image.
Inline SVG for the inserter (matches @wordpress/icons image path).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; upstream init.js / index.php / view.js server-side + interactivity wiring is intentionally absent.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx omits the upstream blob-URL upload mount-effect (useUploadMediaFromBlobURL). Practical impact: drag-and-dropped image files are not eagerly uploaded as soon as they hit the block; users upload via MediaPlaceholder or MediaReplaceFlow as usual.
edit.tsx does not branch on `window.wp.media.view.settings.defaultProps.link` when resolving linkDestination. Newly selected images do not pre-fill `href`; users set it explicitly via the link UI.
edit.tsx does not auto-convert multi-file drops into a gallery block. Multi-file selection emits a snackbar notice instead.

image-editor.tsx replaces upstream's shared `Caption` component (from `@wordpress/block-library/src/utils/caption`) with an inline RichText figcaption because the package's `exports` field does not expose utils/caption.
image-editor.tsx omits `useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps` (a `utils/hooks` private helper) on its ToolsPanel — defaults are used instead.

image-editor.tsx omits the upstream toolbar/inspector tools that depend on `block-editor` private APIs (`DimensionsTool`, `ResolutionTool`, `ImageURLInputUI`, `ImageEditor`/cropper, `FocalPointPicker`, `ResizableBox`, `MediaControl`, `BlockSettingsMenuControls`, the lightbox toggle UI). Their corresponding attributes (`width`, `height`, `aspectRatio`, `scale`, `focalPoint`, `sizeSlug`, `linkDestination`, `linkClass`, `linkTarget`, `rel`, `lightbox`) still serialize via save.tsx and round-trip through transforms; consumers can populate them via the block's stored attributes.

edit.tsx omits the upstream `pickRelevantMediaFiles` size resolution for newly selected media. The fork records the raw `media.url` returned by MediaPlaceholder; consumers wanting size-variant selection should add a sizeSlug control downstream.
